title: The Fruit Bowl That Changed Everything

content.setup: I used to keep fruit in the crisper drawer and cookies on the counter. Then I wondered why I kept eating cookies instead of fruit. I thought I had a willpower problem.
content.development: One day I read about environment design. The idea is simple: we're lazy by design. We eat what we see. We do what's easiest. So I flipped the script - fruit bowl on the counter, cookies hidden in a high cabinet.
content.turning_point: Within a week, my fruit consumption doubled and my cookie consumption dropped by 80%. I didn't try harder. I didn't use more willpower. I just changed my environment.
content.lesson: You don't have to be stronger than your environment. You can redesign it instead. Make good habits obvious and easy. Make bad habits invisible and hard.
content.application: Look around your space right now. What's your environment making easy? What's it making hard? Small changes in setup create big changes in behavior.
